# MODEL: ALL rows use REDUCED 6-layer model (reduced_config_gemma4.yaml).
# NOTE: iter0-iter59 measured WITHOUT --use-server-token-count (client overcounts by ~16%, TPOT appears lower). iter60+ measured WITH --use-server-token-count.

mlir_shape_prop: tpot@c1 -7.8% worse

fuse_add_rms_norm+no_mlir: tpot@c1 -3.6% worse; ttft@c1 +52% much worse
profile run (profiling overhead active, not perf data)

no_piecewise+no_mlir: tpot@c1 -3.6% worse (c256 -5.0% better)

column14: piecewise_num_tokens=32: CRASH (piecewise_num_tokens must be a list, not int)
iter39: adaptive Triton/flashinfer dispatch — tpot@c1 -1.5% vs iter25, c16 flat, c256 +3.8% (investigate)
iter40: dual_norm+norm_add2 — tpot@c1 +4.0%, c16 +8.2% REGRESSION; root cause: dual_norm serializes multi_stream_moe (both MLP+MoE streams now wait for 1 fused op)
iter41: fused QKV norm (tuple custom_op) — tpot@c1 +137%, c16 +295% REGRESSION; root cause: tuple-return custom_op not captured in CUDA graph, 30 graph exit/reentry per decode step
iter42-wrong-model: accidentally ran FULL 30-layer model instead of 6-layer reduced (multi_stream_moe=30 vs 6 expected); results not comparable to baseline
iter42 (correct 6L run): fuse dense+MoE add with post-ffn norm — tpot@c1 -3.8%, c16 -2.7%, c256 +2.5% (src: remove 1 Python dispatch/layer in dynamic eager segments)
iter43: skip aux CPU sync (AD_SKIP_AUX_CPU_SYNC=1) — tpot@c1 -0.3% marginal (< 1.5% threshold)
iter44: fuse post_attn_norm+pre_ff_norm → packed kernel — tpot@c1 -1.8%, c16 +1.7% (within noise), c256 +0.9%

iter45: cross-layer fusion (cross-layer input_layernorm) — tpot@c1 +7.1% REGRESSION, MMLU 34.1% (near-random); root cause: 2-reduction kernel has higher register pressure (worse occupancy) than 2 separate kernels; DISCARD

iter46: extend post_norm_add_and_pre_ff_norm to 3-output (pre_ff_2 via shared inv_rms2) — tpot@c1 +1.92% REGRESSION, TTFT +2.76%; root cause: large-T copy_ overhead + extended Triton kernel memory pressure outweigh savings; DISCARD

iter47: fuse pre_feedforward_layernorm_2 INTO router kernel — tpot@c1 +2.1% REGRESSION vs iter44; high run variability (2nd run p50=2.183 vs 1st run 2.131); router second H-loop may cause cache pressure; DISCARD

iter48: fuse post_feedforward_layernorm_2 INTO combine kernel (gemma4_post_moe_norm_add_norm_add_scale) — stacks on iter47; no additional regression vs iter47; combined iter47+iter48 +2.1% REGRESSION; DISCARD
iter49: 3-reduction combine kernel (fuse post_ff_ln_1+post_ff_ln_2) — full-model tpot_c1=5.418ms (+0.75% vs iter44 5.459ms), below 1.5% threshold; reduced-model -1.07% regression; DISCARD

column1: # --- From iter49 onward: using FULL 30-layer model (examples/auto_deploy/model_registry/configs/gemma4_moe.yaml) ---
# --- Previous rows (iter0-iter48) used reduced 6-layer config (reduced_config_gemma4.yaml); absolute tpot values not comparable ---

column14: iter50: revert iter47 router + keep iter49 combine — full-model tpot_c1=5.406ms (+0.97% vs iter44), below 1.5% threshold; reduced-model tpot_c1=2.162ms (REGRESSION vs iter44 2.139ms); DISCARD

column1: # --- From iter51 onward: back to reduced 6-layer config (reduced_config_gemma4.yaml); iter49-50 full-model experiment conclusive (both DISCARD) ---
# --- iter51-redux (extend post_norm_add_and_pre_ff_norm to 3-output) ABORTED: would duplicate iter46 (already DISCARDed) ---

column14: iter51: fused QKV RMSNorm (q_norm+k_norm+v_norm → 1 Triton launch) — final v3/v4 (fixed code): tpot_c1 avg=2.174ms (+1.62% REGRESSION vs iter44 2.139ms); root cause: 32-block Triton kernel with 3-way conditional + small tiles slower than 3 flashinfer calls; DISCARD

iter52: extend post_norm_add_and_pre_ff_norm to dense layers — avg tpot_c1=2.159ms (+0.92% REGRESSION vs iter44 2.139ms, 4 runs: -0.80/+1.33/+1.86/+1.28%); root cause: dense pre_ff_norm was already in CUDA graph (fast native op), fusion replaced it with slower Triton dual-reduction; DISCARD

column1: # --- From iter53 onward: CPU scheduling overhead reduction; iter44 is the best prior GPU-kernel commit ---

column14: iter55+56: batched cache lookup (n>=64) + get_last_tokens (1-call vs 2) + input_pos cache — c1 flat (2.267ms), c256 -3.6% vs iter44 (18.003ms)
iter57: remove redundant position_ids computation in nest_sequences — c1 flat (2.250ms), c256 -4.0% vs iter44 (17.925ms); accuracy PASSED
iter58: lower batch_cache threshold 64→2 — c256 +5.1% REGRESSION (18.834ms); DISCARD
iter59: cache max_beam_num_tokens for overlap path (dict-based) — c256 +2.5% REGRESSION (18.368ms); DISCARD
iter60: fused_add_rmsnorm for large-T gemma4_post_add_norm_add_scale — eliminates a+b→x temp read, 4→3 kernels, 0 temp allocs; BEST STATE
iter61: rmsnorm out= for all 3 large-T paths — eliminates 1-2 T×H temp allocs + better L2 reuse; c1 -3.2% (2.210→2.140ms), c16 -0.3% noise, c256 -0.7% noise; accuracy PASSED; BEST STATE
iter62: revert iter55+56 — confirmed NO REAL EFFECT (WITH=2.14ms vs WITHOUT=2.12ms, within noise); apparent regression vs iter61 was apples-to-oranges (different token counting); DISCARD

iter63: raise garbage_collection_gen0_threshold 20000->200000 — improved vs fresh same-day baseline, but slower than iter61 at c1/c16 with the same token-counting method; c256-only win is not enough to replace iter61 as best state; DISCARD

column1: # --- NOTE: raw on-disk iter61 artifacts (260410_1127_260410_iter61_v1, 260410_1133_260410_iter61_v2, 260410_1219_iter61_1k1k) do not match row54 exactly. Fresh reproductions are logged below and should be treated as source of truth until reconciled. ---

column14: iter64-base: fresh same-day rerun of iter61 state used for A/B on piecewise bucket sweeps
iter64a: explicit piecewise buckets [64,128,256,512,1000,2000,4000,8192] — same-day c1/c16 better than iter64-base, but still slower than iter61 branch baseline; DISCARD

iter66a: piecewise buckets [64,128,256,512,1000,1536,2048,4096,8192] — no overall win; DISCARD
iter66b: piecewise buckets [64,128,256,512,1000,1536,2048,3072,4096,8192] — c256 win, but c1/c16 regressed too much; DISCARD

iter69b: confirm piecewise [64,128,256,512,1000,1536,2048,3072,4096,8192] — same-day c256 win, but slower than iter61 at c1/c16; DISCARD
iter70: token_gather cache + piecewise [64,128,256,512,1000,1536,2048,3072,4096,8192] — combined regression; DISCARD
iter71: decode-only fast path in ad_executor — same-day win vs iter64-base, but slower than iter61 at c1/c16; DISCARD
iter72: decode fast path + piecewise [64,128,256,512,1000,1536,2048,3072,4096,8192] — regression; DISCARD
iter73: full-model validation of iter71 decode fast path — accuracy PASSED, but c1/c16 slower than same-day full baseline and c256 never completed cleanly; DISCARD
iter74: decode fast path + py_last_token cache — c1 regression; c256 export incomplete; DISCARD
iter75: decode-only SequenceInfo fast path — same-day win vs iter64-base, but still slower than iter61 branch baseline at c1/c16; accuracy PASSED; DISCARD
iter76: same-day full-model baseline for iter73/iter79 comparison; c256 never completed cleanly
iter77: decode fast path + no-spec draft classification shortcut — slower than iter61 at all 3 concurrencies; DISCARD
iter78: decode fast path + SequenceInfo fast path combo — c1 already regressed and run quality was unstable; DISCARD
iter79: full-model validation of iter75 SequenceInfo fast path — accuracy PASSED, but c1/c16 slower than iter76 full baseline; DISCARD
iter80: fresh rebench of ad6e010958 revert iter55+56 — does not reproduce row54 and loses to fresh exact iter61 reproduction on c16/c256; DISCARD
iter81: fresh rebench of current branch (code-identical to 71014e1c8b for perf-relevant files) — does not reproduce row54; DISCARD
iter82: exact 71014e1c8b reproduction under reduced load — reproducible current baseline is 2.215/3.287/18.131, which does not match row54
iter83: piecewise 1010 bucket [64,128,256,512,1010,2048,4096,8192] — c1 regression vs fresh exact iter61 reproduction; stopped early and discarded
iter84: piecewise [64,128,256,512,1000,1010,1536,2048,3072,4096,8192] — c1 regression vs fresh exact iter61 reproduction; stopped early and discarded

column1: # --- Full model baselines resumed; iter86+ uses full 30-layer model (gemma4_moe.yaml) ---

column14: iter86: full-model baseline on current branch (641e3aca63) — c1=5.613ms, c16=12.770ms, c256=19.789ms; TTFT@c256=85.9s due to queuing from slow full-model prefill; reproducible full-model baseline

iter88: multi_stream_gemm transform + piecewise — confirmed no-op for BF16 (matches=0 in server log), c1=2.270ms within noise of 2.215ms baseline; router multi-SM sweep also showed 65% slower (36us vs 22us); DISCARD

iter89: full model + explicit piecewise_num_tokens [64,128,256,512,1000,1536,2048,3072,4096,8192] — c1=5.595ms (+0.32% vs 5.613ms baseline, within noise); piecewise_num_tokens does NOT help full model meaningfully; DISCARD

iter90: reduced-model T-adaptive router (bh=256,nw=4 for T>=256) — c1=2.217ms/c16=3.300ms flat (within noise, expected; router hidden under dense MLP at small T), c256=18.059ms vs 18.080ms baseline (-0.1%, within noise; only 6 layers so 6x13us=78us savings too small to measure); DISCARD

iter91: full-model T-adaptive router (bh=256,nw=4 for T>=256) — c1=5.606ms (-0.1%), c16=12.725ms (-0.4%), c256=19.755ms (-0.2% vs 5.613/12.770/19.789ms baselines); all within noise; standalone benchmark shows 32.4us vs 45.6us at T=256 (-29%), but full-model noise at c256 masked by KV cache pressure (1.7GB free); change is correct (H=2816/256=11 exact, no masked loads); KEEP

iter92: reduced-model _TRITON_T_THRESHOLD=0 (always FlashInfer) — c1=2.245ms (+1.4%), c16=3.291ms (+0.1%), c256=18.265ms (+0.7%); all regressions vs 2.215/3.287/18.131ms baseline; standalone benchmarks showed FlashInfer 1.4-2x faster but that is serial scheduling overhead not CUDA-graph overhead; in CG Triton 1-kernel beats FlashInfer 2-3-kernels due to lower dispatch overhead; REVERTED to threshold=8

iter93: attn_backend=flashinfer in reduced_config_gemma4.yaml — c1=2.343ms (+5.8%), c16=3.317ms (+0.9%), c256=18.199ms (+0.4%); FlashInfer 0.6.6 is broken for Gemma4 on H100: head_dim=512 global layers cause SIGFPE in plan() via fa2 backend (divide-by-zero); local layers (N_H=16,N_KV=8,H=256,GQA=2) get group_size=0 error; both failures leave decode wrappers in corrupted state causing slow fallback path; FlashInfer is definitively NOT viable for Gemma4; REVERTED to triton_paged

iter94: fused QKV-norm: gemma4_qkv_norm custom op replacing 3 separate torch_rmsnorm calls for q/k/v in Gemma4TextAttention; single Triton kernel for total_rows<=256 (BS<=8), flashinfer fallback for large T; CUDA-graph standalone: 3 flashinfer=3.9µs → 1 fused=1.3µs per attn block (3.0x), expected 78µs savings/step over 30 layers; reduced 6-layer model c1=2.214ms flat (6-layer savings ~16µs too small to measure); full model iter95 shows -2.4% → KEEP

iter95: full-model benchmark of iter94 (fused QKV-norm); c1=5.470ms vs 5.606ms iter91-ref (-2.4%=136µs), c16=12.756ms vs 12.725ms (+0.2%, within noise), c256=19.691ms vs 19.755ms (-0.3%, within noise); standalone CUDA-graph bench projected 78µs; actual 136µs (difference may be compile effects); KEEP pending re-run confirmation
